title: Vatica schefferi
intro: Vatica schefferi, also known as the Borneo ironwood, is a species of tree in the family Dipterocarpaceae. The health benefits of Vatica schefferi include its potential anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of arthritis, reduce oxidative stress, and combat infections. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which have been identified as responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Vatica schefferi is commonly prepared into decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, digestive issues, and skin problems.
taxonomy: Vatica schefferi belongs to the family Dipterocarpaceae, which is a group of tropical trees commonly found in Southeast Asia. This species is classified under the genus Vatica, which is a genus of small to medium-sized trees native to the tropical forests of Asia. Further classification places it under the order Malvales and the subclass Rosidae. The family Dipterocarpaceae is a group of flowering plants that are often referred to as 'drought deciduous' trees due to their unique ability to shed their leaves in response to dry conditions. These trees are known for their distinctive leaf patterns and large seeds.
